message:
fix(e2e): isolate deploy-dashboard mock state per-test (Fixes #9087) (#9249)

The deploy-dashboard spec used module-level `let apiCallLog` and
`let deployPhase` variables that route handlers closed over. Tests
mutated `deployPhase` mid-test (e.g. test 9 sets it to 'failed'),
which leaked into other tests when execution order changed —
test 8 could see a stale 'failed' value and get the wrong mock data.

Replace the module-level state with a per-test `MockState` object
created in a Playwright fixture (`mockState`). Route handlers close
over the per-test state, so each test gets a fresh `apiCallLog`
and `deployPhase` with no possibility of cross-test leakage.

The fixture is `auto: true` so it runs for every test in the file
even when the test body doesn't destructure `mockState`, preserving
the previous `beforeEach`-driven behavior of always installing the
mock routes.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
